_about: Mentor write-ups layered on the objective data. `regime` is now decoded exactly from the tick cache (regime_decode.py) and is OBJECTIVE; `regime_override` here only corrects the one 'untagged' session the decoder can't classify (earliest cached day, no prior ATR). The loader applies these via --seed on first-create OR to any still-unreviewed note, and NEVER overwrites a reviewed note. Sizing verdicts use the KB-06 tier system only (Tier-0 = 4 micros ~$40/trade; 1 mini = $100 = hard ceiling; 3 minis = $300 = explicit 'do not'; >=5 minis = off-tier experiment). Kill-rule = 2-3 consecutive stops -> done (KB-06 daily-loss). Grades weigh discipline (kill-rule, in-window %, overtrading, sizing tier, giveback) over raw $, and discount sessions whose P&L is a size artifact.

20260609_132716_1c17f2.psych_during: Reads like tilt — 15 straight fades into a down-trend, no manual cuts, just stop after stop (10 STOP / 5 TRAIL).
20260609_132716_1c17f2.what_went_right: All 15 entries were inside the 13-15 locked window — the only thing that was right.
20260609_132716_1c17f2.what_went_wrong: Worst R of the early batch (-5.5R), -$1824, 33% WR, dir-skill -57, max DD -$2171. Hit 4 consecutive stops and KEPT TRADING — a clear kill-rule breach (KB-06: 2-3 stops = done). Gave back through a green peak. Size 3 minis = $300/trade = the explicit KB-06 'do not'.
20260609_132716_1c17f2.lesson: THE session to study. Trend-down + pure fade + no kill-switch = max damage. The market said 'wrong tool' by the 3rd stop; 12 more trades followed. One rule saves it: after 3 stops, the session is over.
20260609_132716_1c17f2.note: Real date 2025-10-30 (Thu). Tick-cache 'untagged' (earliest cached session, no prior ATR baseline); regime_override set to trend-down from the RTH close direction.

20260607_172504_472b10.psych_during: Worked a trend-down day for a thin green, but got loose late (3 stops, kept going).
20260607_172504_472b10.what_went_right: Net +$330 on a down-trend day, mostly fading (12/13), 10/13 in-window, size held at 1 mini.
20260607_172504_472b10.what_went_wrong: Only +0.6R for the day — a lot of activity for little edge. Hit 3 consecutive stops and continued (kill-rule breach), then gave back through the peak.
20260607_172504_472b10.lesson: Green on a trend-down day is fine, but +0.6R across 13 trades is a grind. Respect the 3-stop stop and bank the green instead of round-tripping it.
20260607_172504_472b10.note: Real date 2025-11-03 (Mon). 1 mini = $100/trade = KB-06 hard ceiling (above Tier-0's 4 micros).

20260607_174134_37cde4.psych_during: Confident and in-flow on a clean trend-up day — let winners work (PF 1.58).
20260607_174134_37cde4.what_went_right: +3.8R, PF 1.58, max 2 consecutive stops (kill-rule respected), good fade+aligned balance (21/10) on a trending day. Size disciplined at 1 mini.
20260607_174134_37cde4.what_went_wrong: 31 trades is heavy overtrading, and only 13/31 were in the locked windows — a lot of marginal out-of-window reps diluting a strong day.
20260607_174134_37cde4.lesson: Your best raw edge days come from trend-up. Keep the kill-rule discipline; cut the trade count and out-of-window reps and this is an A.
20260607_174134_37cde4.note: Real date 2025-11-05 (Wed). 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.

20260607_191605_4ccb33.psych_during: The +$1186 feels good but it's a size mirage — a 10-lot swing carried a losing-quality session.
20260607_191605_4ccb33.what_went_right: Net green and the fade/aligned mix (9/4) was reasonable on a trend-up day.
20260607_191605_4ccb33.what_went_wrong: Edge was NEGATIVE (-1.0R) — the green is purely the 10-mini size, not skill. 10 minis = $1,000/trade = wildly off-tier (KB-06 ceiling is 1 mini). 4 consecutive stops with trading continued = kill-rule breach.
20260607_191605_4ccb33.lesson: Dollars from size are not edge. At a tier-compliant size this is a red day. Judge the session on R and discipline, not the inflated $.
20260607_191605_4ccb33.note: Real date 2025-11-10 (Mon). SIZE EXPERIMENT (1 & 10 minis) — P&L not representative.

20260607_201809_b21bea.psych_during: Calm and patient — max 1 consecutive stop all session is excellent emotional control.
20260607_201809_b21bea.what_went_right: +1.6R, PF 1.73, NEVER took 2 stops in a row (best kill-rule discipline of the batch), size held at 1 mini, balanced fade/aligned (11/6) on a trend-up day.
20260607_201809_b21bea.what_went_wrong: 17 trades is mildly over, and 10/17 in-window leaves some out-of-zone reps. Minor.
20260607_201809_b21bea.lesson: This is the discipline template for a positive day: never let losses cluster, let PF do the work. Replicate the 'one stop and reset' rhythm.
20260607_201809_b21bea.note: Real date 2025-12-05 (Fri). 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.

20260607_200645_ef035e.psych_during: This is a size stress-test, not trading — sizes run 1 / 5 / 50 / 500 minis in one session.
20260607_200645_ef035e.what_went_right: Nothing about this is a discipline rep. It demonstrates how size warps the P&L.
20260607_200645_ef035e.what_went_wrong: +$3508 is a pure artifact of trading up to 500 minis; the actual edge was -4.6R (deeply negative). 500 minis = ~$50k risk/trade = a catastrophic KB-06 violation (ceiling is 1 mini). 3 consecutive stops, continued.
20260607_200645_ef035e.lesson: Quarantine size experiments from the journal's edge read. At any tier-compliant size this is a blow-up; the headline $ is meaningless.
20260607_200645_ef035e.note: Real date 2025-12-08 (Mon). SIZE EXPERIMENT (up to 500 minis) — P&L is NOT a trading result. Excluded mentally from edge.

20260606_233936_5a86a8.psych_during: Aimless — 22 trades for a scratch, almost entirely outside the locked windows.
20260606_233936_5a86a8.what_went_right: Stayed green (+$78), max 2 consecutive stops, on a trend-up day.
20260606_233936_5a86a8.what_went_wrong: Only 2 of 22 entries were in-window — near-total window-discipline failure. +0.4R is a flat coinflip with fees. Mixed 1/5-mini sizing (5 minis = $500 = 25% MLL/trade).
20260606_233936_5a86a8.lesson: Trading all day outside 10-11 / 13-15 turns your edge into noise. The window rule exists precisely to stop sessions like this.
20260606_233936_5a86a8.note: Real date 2025-12-23 (Tue). No flow data (fade/aligned unknown). 5 minis breaches tier.

20260607_181916_99d1b1.psych_during: Handled a high-vol day without blowing up — balanced and steady.
20260607_181916_99d1b1.what_went_right: +1.4R, PF 1.14, survived a high-vol regime (FH/ATR 2.46), kill-rule respected (max 2 stops), size held at 1 mini, healthy fade/aligned balance (15/12).
20260607_181916_99d1b1.what_went_wrong: 27 trades is overtrading, and 11/27 in-window. High-vol days reward patience, not volume.
20260607_181916_99d1b1.lesson: Good that high-vol didn't tilt you. Trim the count — on wide-range days fewer, better-located fades beat grinding 27 reps.
20260607_181916_99d1b1.note: Real date 2026-01-29 (Thu). 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.

20260607_235751_5b1551.psych_during: Patient enough to scratch green but clearly hunting — 22 trades to net +$53, riding a -$1784 drawdown.
20260607_235751_5b1551.what_went_right: Stayed net positive (+1.1R) on a trend-down day, fading throughout (21/22).
20260607_235751_5b1551.what_went_wrong: Hit 4 consecutive stops and kept trading (kill-rule breach). Only 6/22 entries in-window. Size 3 minis = $300 = the KB-06 'do not'. Heat-to-reward was awful (DD -$1784 for +$53).
20260607_235751_5b1551.lesson: A tiny green that required a huge drawdown and a kill-rule breach is not a good day. Fewer, in-window fades hold the same green with a quarter of the heat.
20260607_235751_5b1551.note: Real date 2026-02-10 (Tue). Regime corrected from my earlier 'range-chop' read to trend-down (exact tick-cache decode).

20260607_223830_21b7eb.psych_during: Choppy and a bit loose — 4 stops in a row on a trend-up day, ended slightly red.
20260607_223830_21b7eb.what_went_right: Roughly breakeven (+0.3R), size held at 1 mini, reasonable fade/aligned (13/3).
20260607_223830_21b7eb.what_went_wrong: -$32 net, PF 0.97, and a 4-consecutive-stop run with trading continued (kill-rule breach). 9/16 in-window.
20260607_223830_21b7eb.lesson: Breakeven masking a 4-stop cluster is a discipline miss, not a neutral day. The kill-rule would have ended it red but smaller.
20260607_223830_21b7eb.note: Real date 2026-02-13 (Fri). 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.

20260606_221031_2c20d2.psych_during: Wrong-footed but disciplined about it — took the loss and stopped at 9 trades.
20260606_221031_2c20d2.what_went_right: Respected trade-count discipline (only 9 trades, max 2 consecutive stops) — did NOT compound a losing read. That restraint is the redeeming feature.
20260606_221031_2c20d2.what_went_wrong: -$478, PF 0.45, -2.2R — a clearly losing session on a trend-up day. No flow data to grade fade quality.
20260606_221031_2c20d2.lesson: A red day where you didn't tilt is recoverable. This is what a controlled loss looks like vs. 1c17f2 — same wrong read, a fraction of the damage.
20260606_221031_2c20d2.note: Real date 2026-02-17 (Tue). 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.

20260607_002115_06ebb2.psych_during: Death by a thousand cuts — 36 trades grinding a trend-down day into the ground.
20260607_002115_06ebb2.what_went_right: Kill-rule technically respected (max 2 consecutive stops) and 28/36 in-window — the discipline that existed was mechanical.
20260607_002115_06ebb2.what_went_wrong: Worst R of all 21 sessions (-8.3R), -$1316, PF 0.38. 36 trades is the most overtraded session in the set, at 5 minis ($500 = 25% MLL/trade). The kill-rule on consecutive stops didn't fire, but a daily-loss limit should have ended this far earlier.
20260607_002115_06ebb2.lesson: Consecutive-stop counting isn't enough — you also need the KB-06 DAILY-LOSS cap ($200 / ~half the banked cushion). 36 trades bleeding -8.3R is exactly what that cap is for.
20260607_002115_06ebb2.note: Real date 2026-02-23 (Mon). 5 minis breaches tier. No flow data.

20260607_190316_f2b259.psych_during: Another size mirage — +$2960 headline on a negative-edge, kill-rule-breaching session.
20260607_190316_f2b259.what_went_right: Good fade/aligned balance (10/9) on a trend-down day; the green looks great on paper.
20260607_190316_f2b259.what_went_wrong: Edge was -3.1R — the +$2960 is the 10-mini size, not skill. 10 minis = ~$1,000/trade = grossly off-tier. 4 consecutive stops with trading continued = kill-rule breach.
20260607_190316_f2b259.lesson: Same trap as 4ccb33/ef035e: a big green from size hides a red-quality, undisciplined session. Strip the size and grade the behavior.
20260607_190316_f2b259.note: Real date 2026-03-10 (Tue). SIZE EXPERIMENT (1 & 10 minis) — P&L not representative.

20260607_210139_3b0bdf.psych_during: Locked in — this is your edge expressed cleanly. 20 fades on a trend-up day, losses never clustered.
20260607_210139_3b0bdf.what_went_right: Best R of all 21 sessions (+7.5R), PF 1.75, max 2 consecutive stops (kill-rule respected), size held at 1 mini. Fade-heavy (20/2) and it paid.
20260607_210139_3b0bdf.what_went_wrong: 22 trades is a touch high and 11/22 in-window — the only blemish. Trim out-of-window reps and it's a clean A.
20260607_210139_3b0bdf.lesson: This + b21bea are the templates: trend-up, fade your spots, never let stops cluster, hold size. Note it for replication.
20260607_210139_3b0bdf.note: Real date 2026-03-16 (Mon). This is also the locked validation-baseline anchor session. 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.

20260607_215512_dfc425.psych_during: Steady — fading a trend-down day and actually making it work for once.
20260607_215512_dfc425.what_went_right: +2.1R, PF 1.19, green on a trend-down day (where fade usually bleeds), kill-rule respected (max 2 stops), size held at 1 mini, fade-heavy (14/1).
20260607_215512_dfc425.what_went_wrong: Only 6/15 in-window — most of the edge came from out-of-window trades, which isn't repeatable under the rules.
20260607_215512_dfc425.lesson: Proof fade CAN win on a down day with good location. Pull those reps into the windows so the result is rule-compliant.
20260607_215512_dfc425.note: Real date 2026-03-25 (Wed). 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.

20260607_122631_a5c7ff.psych_during: Sharp and selective — highest profit factor of the set, almost no stop clustering.
20260607_122631_a5c7ff.what_went_right: PF 3.24 (best of all 21), +3.5R, max 1 consecutive stop (elite kill-rule discipline), size held at 1 mini, fade-heavy (16/2) on a trend-down day.
20260607_122631_a5c7ff.what_went_wrong: Only 5/18 in-window — the one real knock. The edge was excellent but largely booked outside the locked windows.
20260607_122631_a5c7ff.lesson: A-quality execution on the wrong clock. Same patience and location inside 10-11 / 13-15 and this is your model session.
20260607_122631_a5c7ff.note: Real date 2026-03-26 (Thu). 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.

20260609_133346_77a655.psych_during: Calm and selective — this is what good looks like for you. 10 trades, all in-window, all fade, clean.
20260609_133346_77a655.what_went_right: +$429, PF 1.32, +1.8R, dir-skill +80, max DD only -$683. 10 disciplined fades on a trend-up day, EVERY entry inside the 13-15 window, kill-rule respected (max 2 stops). One well-timed manual exit.
20260609_133346_77a655.what_went_wrong: Almost nothing on execution. The single blemish is size: 3 minis = $300 = the KB-06 'do not' — at a tier-compliant size this same session is an A.
20260609_133346_77a655.lesson: The template for SELECTION: trend-up + fade + tight rep count + in-window only. Fix the size to Tier-0 and it's flawless.
20260609_133346_77a655.note: Real date 2026-04-06 (Mon) PM. Regime corrected from my earlier 'range-chop' read to trend-up (exact decode). Same calendar day as 9a85aa but a far more disciplined session.

20260608_001446_9a85aa.psych_during: Grind mode — 38 trades is a marathon. Didn't blow up, but this is volume for volume's sake.
20260608_001446_9a85aa.what_went_right: Net +$196 / +2.2R, best in-window discipline of the batch (23/38), fade-heavy (33/5) on a trend-up day.
20260608_001446_9a85aa.what_went_wrong: 38 trades is the 2nd-most overtraded session here. Hit 3 consecutive stops and continued (kill-rule breach) and gave back through a green peak. Size 3 minis = the KB-06 'do not'.
20260608_001446_9a85aa.lesson: PF barely above 1.0 across 38 trades is a coinflip with fee drag. Cut the count hard, honor the 3-stop kill, drop to Tier-0 size.
20260608_001446_9a85aa.note: Real date 2026-04-06 (Mon) AM. Regime corrected from 'range-chop' to trend-up (exact decode).

20260606_222356_f407d2.psych_during: Frustrating — the one true range-chop day, and it bled instead of paying.
20260606_222356_f407d2.what_went_right: Kill-rule respected (max 2 stops) and 20/25 in-window — mechanically disciplined.
20260606_222356_f407d2.what_went_wrong: -$1100, PF 0.38, -5.0R on the ONLY range-chop session in the set. 25 trades overtrading. No flow data, so fade quality is unknown — but the result says location/timing was poor.
20260606_222356_f407d2.lesson: Range-chop is NOT an automatic fade win — this is the cleanest counter-evidence to the 'fade loves chop' assumption. Choppy ranges still require good location; volume made it worse.
20260606_222356_f407d2.note: Real date 2026-04-10 (Fri). Only exact range-chop day (FH/ATR 0.46). 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.

20260608_212037_aab3d1.psych_during: Wrong read, kept feeding it — fading hard on a trend-up day that didn't rotate.
20260608_212037_aab3d1.what_went_right: Did size one trade down to 1 mini (the only flicker of the cushion instinct); 18/25 in-window.
20260608_212037_aab3d1.what_went_wrong: -$1372, PF 0.65, -2.9R, dir-skill -24. 24/25 trades were fades and the day trended through them. Hit 3 consecutive stops and continued (kill-rule breach). Size up to 3 minis = the KB-06 'do not'.
20260608_212037_aab3d1.lesson: When dir-skill is negative early, the fade is the wrong tool for the regime — stand down, don't add reps. (Regime corrected: this was trend-UP, not the trend-down I first read.)
20260608_212037_aab3d1.note: Real date 2025-11-10 (Mon). Regime corrected from 'trend-down' to trend-up (exact decode).

20260607_213110_f6a697.psych_during: Tidy and controlled — small green, mostly in-window, stopped at a sensible count.
20260607_213110_f6a697.what_went_right: +$163, PF 1.37, +2.0R, 9/10 in-window, only 10 trades, all fades on a trend-up day. Took 3 stops but STOPPED after (kill-rule effectively respected).
20260607_213110_f6a697.what_went_wrong: Gave back roughly half of a green peak before finishing — the green-giveback rule (KB-06) should have locked it sooner. Size held at 1 mini (ceiling).
20260607_213110_f6a697.lesson: Good small disciplined day. The one tweak: when up and giving back half, stop — protect the green per the giveback rule.
20260607_213110_f6a697.note: Real date 2026-04-22 (Wed). 1 mini = KB-06 ceiling tier.
